CIPHER.1468.cipherString: The most marvelous experience of life is to transform life according to reality, not imagination.
CIPHER.1468.question: A coded message is discovered inside a fortune cookie. It turns out to be a quote by Vernon Howard, encoded using an Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.1468.author: Vernon Howard

CIPHER.1469.cipherString: History is written by the victors ... except on Wikipedia haha
CIPHER.1469.question: Hidden in the source code of a retro video game, this Baconian puzzle reveals an interesting observation. What does it say?
CIPHER.1469.author: Wikipedia

CIPHER.1470.cipherString: Life must not be a novel that is given to us, but one that is made by us.
CIPHER.1470.question: You find this encoded quote written inside the cover of a chemistry reference book. It's attributed to Novalis, encoded as a Complete Columnar. The quote has NOVEL somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.1470.author: Novalis

CIPHER.1471.cipherString: Who are we? We are the life force power of the universe.
CIPHER.1471.question: You uncover this encoded note tucked behind a microscope slide. It's a quote by Jill Bolte Taylor, written in a Baconian. What does it say?
CIPHER.1471.author: Jill Bolte Taylor

CIPHER.1472.question: Your microscope slide prep strangely revealed this Hill encoded message by Audre Lorde with a keyword of THRU. What does it say?
CIPHER.1472.author: Audre Lorde

CIPHER.1473.cipherString: Las dudas en tu mente son un obstáculo mucho mayor para el éxito que los obstáculos en el viaje.
CIPHER.1473.question: You intercept a mysterious radio signal. Decoding it reveals a quote by Orrin Woodward in Spanish encrypted as a Xenocrypt using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.1473.author: Orrin Woodward
CIPHER.1473.translation: Doubts in your mind are a much greater roadblock to success than obstacles on the journey.

CIPHER.1474.question: Someone left this encrypted quote scribbled on the chalkboard before class began. It's a quote, written as a Cryptarithm. What do the values 98 73458 23106 decode to?

CIPHER.1475.cipherString: It is more important to discover a new source of happiness on earth than a new planet in the sky.
CIPHER.1475.question: A message in a bottle washed up in the school's decorative fountain—decode this Aristocrat cipher text. What does it say?
CIPHER.1475.author: Unknown

CIPHER.1476.cipherString: Much corn lies under the straw that is not seen.
CIPHER.1476.question: The bottom of the trophy has a Proverb engraved in a Fractionated Morse cipher. You are told that the quote has CORN in it corresponding to the encoded text H D L F V Y, meaning D = ●×–; F = ●–●; H = –●–; L = ––×; V = ×–●; Y = ××●. What does it say?
CIPHER.1476.author: Proverb

CIPHER.1477.cipherString: Quality in a classical Greek sense is how to live with grace and intelligence, with bravery and mercy.
CIPHER.1477.question: A message was left on the whiteboard after science club. It's a quote by Theodore White encoded using an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.1477.author: Theodore White

CIPHER.1478.cipherString: I always say centered food equals centered behavior.
CIPHER.1478.question: An explorer's journal mentions a “hidden truth” encoded below. It turns out to be a quote by Marilu Henner, written in a Nihilist Substitution Cipher with a keyword of INGEST and polybius key of COUNTRYSIDE. What does it say?
CIPHER.1478.author: Marilu Henner

CIPHER.1479.cipherString: It's like building a cathedral, in which only a few seconds of animation are completed each week.
CIPHER.1479.question: A time-capsule USB drive from 1983 just auto-opened with this Aristocrat-encrypted message using a K2 alphabet. What was the K2 Keyword used to encode it?

CIPHER.1480.cipherString: Part of filmmaking is always a guessing game, and part of it is always a game of trust.
CIPHER.1480.question: The school's ancient overhead projector flickered and burned this Checkerboard message onto the screen—decode Greg Kinnear's quote with a polybius key of WORKMANSHIP. What does it say?
CIPHER.1480.author: Greg Kinnear

CIPHER.1481.cipherString: Effective communication is the compass of leadership, guiding the way to collective success.
CIPHER.1481.question: A glitch in the school's grading portal displayed this Aristocrat string by Felecia Etienne. You are told that the cipher contains the word THE twice.  What does it say?
CIPHER.1481.author: Felecia Etienne

CIPHER.1482.cipherString: No fool was ever so foolish, but some one thought him clever.
CIPHER.1482.question: Your locker door has a Proverb scratched in a Complete Columnar cipher. The quote has FOOLI somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.1482.author: Proverb

CIPHER.1483.cipherString: Music, when healthy, is the teacher of perfect order; and also when depraved, the teacher of perfect disorder.
CIPHER.1483.question: The marching band's sheet music rearranged itself into this Aristocrat pattern—it's a quote by Ruskin encoded using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains the word when twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.1483.author: Ruskin

CIPHER.1484.cipherString: If bread is the first necessity of life, recreation is a close second.
CIPHER.1484.question: An old robot prototype suddenly prints this encoded text. It's a quote by Edward Bellamy, encrypted using a Porta using a keyword of PARODY. What does it say?
CIPHER.1484.author: Edward Bellamy

CIPHER.1485.cipherString: There is no great invention, from fire to flying, which has not been hailed as an insult to some god.
CIPHER.1485.question: You intercepted this transmission from a rogue weather balloon. Decode the Aristocrat version of J. B. S. Haldane's famous words. You are told that the cipher contains the word TO twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.1485.author: J. B. S. Haldane

CIPHER.1486.cipherString: In all of living, have much fun and laughter. Life is to be enjoyed, not just endured.
CIPHER.1486.question: Someone passed you a piece of paper with this Checkerboard encoded quote by Gordon B. Hinckley with a polybius key of SCREAMING. What does it say?
CIPHER.1486.author: Gordon B. Hinckley

CIPHER.1487.cipherString: Now that I'm a free agent I mean to make my own choices, and explain them to nobody if that's what pleases me.
CIPHER.1487.question: During a field trip, you notice these strange markings on a metal plaque. They represent a quote by James Blish, encoded using an Aristocrat Cipher using a K1 alphabet. What was the K1 Keyword used to encode it?
CIPHER.1487.author: James Blish

CIPHER.1488.question: A vending machine just dispensed a candy wrapper with this Cryptarithm code. What do the values 4561 32 189250 decode to?

CIPHER.1489.cipherString: A man was found guilty of overusing commas. The judge warned him to expect a really long sentence.
CIPHER.1489.question: A note falls out of a borrowed library book. The message is a quote, written as an Aristocrat Cipher using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.1489.author: Unknown

CIPHER.1490.cipherString: Happiness is a reward that comes to those that have not looked for it.
CIPHER.1490.question: Upon searching a room, the following were found on scraps of paper. You are told it's a quote by Émile Chartier encoded as a Complete Columnar. The quote has THOSE somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.1490.author: Émile Chartier

CIPHER.1491.cipherString: His purposes will ripen fast, Unfolding every hour; The bud may have a bitter taste, But sweet will be the flower.
CIPHER.1491.question: A mysterious postcard arrives from an unknown sender. It contains a coded quote by William Cowper, written in an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains the word will twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.1491.author: William Cowper

CIPHER.1492.cipherString: I'll never have enough time to paint all the pictures I'd like to.
CIPHER.1492.question: Scrawled in dry-erase marker on the whiteboard after hours: this Nihilist Substitution encoded quote by Norman Rockwell with a keyword of PURELY and polybius key of INSOMNIACS. What does it say?
CIPHER.1492.author: Norman Rockwell

CIPHER.1493.cipherString: To conquer fear is the beginning of wisdom.
CIPHER.1493.question: Bertrand Russell offers an observation that has been encoded as a Baconian. You are told that the deciphered text starts with TOCO. What does it say?
CIPHER.1493.author: Bertrand Russell

CIPHER.1494.cipherString: It was one of those perfect English autumnal days which occur more frequently in memory than in life.
CIPHER.1494.question: This code is found written in chalk on the sidewalk outside the science building. It's a quote by P.D. James, written in an Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.1494.author: P.D. James

CIPHER.1497.cipherString: There is no prize to perfection, only an end to pursuit.
CIPHER.1497.question: The following quote by Viktor in Arcane needs to be decoded with the Fractionated Morse Cipher. You are told that the quote has PERF in it corresponding to the encoded text X H T E P G B, meaning B = ●×–; E = ●–●; G = –●×; H = ––●; P = ×●●; T = ×●×; X = ××●. What does it say?
